data.title: The AI Trade could kill stock buybacks
data.description:
The race for AI dominance could soon wipe out one of corporate America's favorite avenues for shareholder return: the stock buyback.

The market may be on the brink of a “de facto Fed tightening,” according to Nomura strategist Charlie McElligott. If growth and inflation reaccelerate — a scenario he says would catch most investors flat-footed — then loose financial conditions could create “such a profound wealth effect via the stock market” that traders would have to price in a less dovish Fed path.

"And if I’m really getting provocative for the Bears," he said. "Add that 'De Facto Fed Tightening' backdrop to a world where the AI theme -- which IS the Stock market -- begins to 1) lean on Credit Spreads (wider) to continue chasing Capex, while 2) the FCF burn too negatively impacts Cash previously available over the past 15 years which was primarily 'THE' source of funds for Corp Share Repurchases."

In other words hello data centers, bye bye buybacks.

The charts below show projected free cash from of hyperscalers Amazon (AMZN), Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL), Meta (META), Microsoft (MSFT) and Oracle (ORCL) and the rising trend in S&P 500 (SPY) (IVV) (VOO) share repurchase programs.

data.title: Lilly, Novo in pact with Mangoceuticals for direct sales of weight loss drugs
data.description:
Mangoceuticals (MGRX) shares rose ~19% in the premarket on Thursday after the telemedicine platform announced partnerships with Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO) to provide direct access to the pharma giants’ GLP-1 weight loss drugs.

As part of the deals, Dallas, Texas-based Mangoceuticals (MGRX) said it has launched MangoRx Direct and PeachesRx Direct to sell LLY’s (LLY) Zepbound (tirzepatide) and Novo’s (NVO) Wegovy (semaglutide) to cash-paying customers.

Additionally, patients can receive virtual consultations, personalized obesity and weight management treatment plans, and other benefits through MangoRx Direct and PeachesRx, the company added.

“For just $99/month, our medication management membership includes unlimited telehealth visits, personalized progress tracking, and lifestyle coaching,” CEO Jacob Cohen noted.

“GLP-1 medications are provided separately at fixed cash-pay pricing through LillyDirect and NovoCare programs,” he added, referring to the companies’ direct-to-consumer platforms.

The launch follows the Trump administration’s recent agreements with Eli Lilly (LLY) and Novo Nordisk (NVO) to reduce prices of the companies’ prescription medicines, including GLP-1 drugs, for Medicare, Medicaid, and self-paying customers.

data.title: Ballard Power Systems GAAP EPS of -$0.09 beats by $0.02, revenue of $32.5M beats by $7.53M
data.description:
• Ballard Power Systems press release (BLDP): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$0.09 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $32.5M (+119.6% Y/Y) beats by $7.53M.
  • Net order intake of $19.1 million.
  • Lower Capital Expenditure outlook range of $8 to $12 million compared to previous estimate of $15-$25 million.

data.title: Elemental Altus, EMX Royalty announce merger completion
data.description:
Elemental Altus Royalties (OTCQX:ELEMF) and EMX Royalty (EMX) said Thursday they closed their previously announced merger agreement following approval by shareholders of both companies and court approval of the transaction earlier this month.

Elemental Altus (OTCQX:ELEMF) said it will file to change its name to Elemental Royalty Corporation, and expects to begin trading November 14 on the TSX Venture Exchange under the ELE ticker symbol and on the OTCQX Best Market under the ELEMF ticker symbol; the company also has filed an application to list its common shares on Nasdaq Stock Market under the ELE symbol.

In connection with the closing, the company appointed David Cole as CEO, Frederick Bell as President and COO, and Stefan Wenger as CFO.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Stratasys FY guides profit and revenue above consensus following strong Q3 beat
Better Home & Finance GAAP EPS of -$2.56 misses by $0.27, revenue of $43.87M misses by $4.19M
data.description:
• Better Home & Finance Holding press release (BETR): Q3 GAAP EPS of -$2.56 misses by $0.27.
  • Revenue of $43.87M (+51.3% Y/Y) misses by $4.19M.
  • Q3 2025 total funded loan volume grew 17% year over year compared to Q3 2024. Excluding funded loan volume from a discontinued partnership in Q3 2024, funded loan volume grew 56% year over year compared to the same quarter in 2024.
  • The Company affirms guidance of achieving Adjusted EBITDA breakeven by the end of Q3 2026

data.title: Via Transportation impresses with its first earnings report as a public company
data.description:
Via Transportation (VIA) highlighted with its first earnings report as a public company that it is pleased with the momentum of the business.

Revenue rose 32% year-over-year to $109.7 million on a customer count that was 11% higher than a year ago. Adjusted gross profit rose 33% to $43.5 million. Via Transportation's (VIA) adjusted net loss for the quarter narrowed by 33% to $9.7 million in comparison to a year ago.

CEO update: "Our investment in innovation and focus on ensuring we deliver quantifiable ROI to our customers has enabled us to exceed expectations across all key growth metrics. At the same time, we continue to generate significant operating leverage in the business, resulting in a continuous improvement to our adjusted EBITDA margin. We also benefit from the unique characteristics of the government sector, which provide high visibility as we look to the quarters ahead.”

Looking ahead, the public transportation software company sees Q4 platform revenue of $114.6 million to $115.1 million and full-year platform revenue of $430 million to $403.5 million.

Via Transportation (VIA) priced its IPO on September 12 at $46 per share. The stock hit a high of $56.31 before settling back. Shares of VIA edged 0.5% higher in premarket trading to $50.75. The market cap on VIA is just over $4 billion.

data.title: Stratasys GAAP EPS of $0.02 beats by $0.30, revenue of $137M beats by $0.4M
data.description:
• Stratasys press release (SSYS): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.02 beats by $0.30.
  • Revenue of $137M (-2.1% Y/Y) beats by $0.4M.
  • FY25 Guidance:
  • Full year revenue of $550 million to $560 million vs $553.31M consensus
  • Full year non-GAAP gross margins of 46.7% to 47.0%.
  • Full year non-GAAP operating expenses to range from $248 million to $251 million.
  • Full year GAAP operating margins to range from (13)% to (11)%.
  • Full year non-GAAP operating margins to range from 1.5% to 2.0%.
  • GAAP net loss ranging from $110 million to $99 million, and GAAP EPS of ($1.34) to ($1.21).
  • Adjusted EBITDA ranging from $30 million to $32 million.
  • Capital expenditures ranging from $20 million to $25 million.
  • Positive operating cash flow.
  • Non-GAAP net income ranging from $11 million to $13 million, and Non-GAAP EPS ranging from $0.13 to $0.16 vs $0.14 consensus.

data.title: Suburban Propane Partners GAAP EPS of -$0.53 beats by $0.01, revenue of $211.38M misses by $15.62M
data.description:
• Suburban Propane Partners press release (SPH): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.53 beats by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $211.38M (+1.3% Y/Y) misses by $15.62M.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025 was $0.7 million, compared to $0.8 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal 2024.  Retail propane gallons sold were 60.8 million gallons for the fourth quarter of fiscal 2025, which increased 1.8% compared to the prior year fourth quarter.

data.title: Centuri announces pricing of public offering of common stock
data.description:
• Centuri Holdings (CTRI) on Thursday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 7.44M shares of Centuri’s common stock.
  • Centuri has also granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.11M shares of Centuri’s common stock.
  • The offering is expected to close on November 14.
  • Gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $160 million.
  • Centuri intends to use the net proceeds from the offering and the private placement for general corporate purposes, which may include, among other things, the funding of acquisitions and the repayment of borrowings outstanding under its current credit agreement.
  • Source: Press Release
